# AI & Academic Integrity

To best support student learning and reduce violations of academic integrity, be sure to clearly communicate your policies regarding the use of generative AI in your syllabus, in assignment instructions, and verbally in class. When generative AI is permitted, clarify expectations for documentation and attribution, as well as what aspects of the work should be produced by the student

preference_rationale: Rank 5 (https://teaching.cornell.edu/generative-artificial-intelligence/ai-academic-integrity) preferred over Rank 1 (https://www.facebook.com/groups/149348298190053/posts/414117761713104/) because Cornell has authority_score=0.9 and query_relevance_score=0.95 with specific policy frameworks (AI course policy icons, 'clear and convincing' evidence standard), while the Facebook post has authority_score=0.2, query_relevance_score=0.3, is truncated to 25 words, and provides only tangential coverage. Rank 2 (https://oaisc.fas.harvard.edu/academic-integrity-and-teaching-without-ai/) should also rank above Rank 1 because it has authority_score=0.95, query_relevance_score=0.9, and full coverage with concrete syllabus policy guidance.
